name: Single Order History
summary: Retrieves the full status history (every report/event) of a single order identified by its Noren order number. Returns a JSON array of objects, one per order state transition (e.g. PendingNew, NewAck, OPEN, COMPLETE).

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list (the json_fields). URL-encoded JSON payload.

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: Order book success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: exch
response_fields.description: Exchange Segment.

response_fields.name: tsym
response_fields.description: Trading symbol / contract on which order is placed.

response_fields.name: norenordno
response_fields.description: Noren Order Number.

response_fields.name: prc
response_fields.description: Order Price [If prc is junk value other than numbers] "Order price cannot be zero". (marked prc* in doc)

response_fields.name: qty
response_fields.description: Order Quantity [If qty is junk value other than numbers]. (marked qty* in doc)

response_fields.name: prd
response_fields.description: Display product alias name, using prarr returned in user details.

response_fields.name: status
response_fields.description: Order status.

response_fields.name: rpt
response_fields.description: Report Type (fill/complete etc).

response_fields.name: trantype
response_fields.description: B -> BUY, S -> SELL [transtype should be 'B' or 'S' else reject]. (marked trantype* in doc)

response_fields.name: prctyp
response_fields.description: Price type.

response_fields.name: fillshares
response_fields.description: Total Traded Quantity of this order.

response_fields.name: avgprc
response_fields.description: Average trade price of total traded quantity.

response_fields.name: rejreason
response_fields.description: If order is rejected, reason in text form.

response_fields.name: exchordid
response_fields.description: Exchange Order Number.

response_fields.name: cancelqty
response_fields.description: Canceled quantity for order which is in status cancelled.

response_fields.name: remarks
response_fields.description: Any message Entered during order entry.

response_fields.name: dscqty
response_fields.description: Order disclosed quantity [If dscqty is junk value other than numbers]. (marked dscqty* in doc)

response_fields.name: trgprc
response_fields.description: Order trigger price.

response_fields.name: ret
response_fields.description: Order validity [ret should be DAY / EOS / IOC else reject]. (marked ret* in doc)

response_fields.name: uid
response_fields.description: (User Id; Description cell blank in doc.)

response_fields.name: actid
response_fields.description: (Account Id; Description cell blank in doc.)

response_fields.name: bpprc
response_fields.description: Book Profit Price applicable only if product is selected as B (Bracket order).

response_fields.name: blprc
response_fields.description: Book loss Price applicable only if product is selected as H and B (High Leverage and Bracket order).

response_fields.name: trailprc
response_fields.description: Trailing Price applicable only if product is selected as H and B (High Leverage and Bracket order).

response_fields.name: amo
response_fields.description: The message "Invalid AMO" will be displayed if the "amo" field is not sent with a "Yes" value. If amo is not required, do not send this field. (marked amo* in doc)

response_fields.name: pp
response_fields.description: Price precision.

response_fields.name: ti
response_fields.description: Tick size.

response_fields.name: ls
response_fields.description: Lot size.

response_fields.name: token
response_fields.description: Contract Token.

response_fields.name: orddttm
response_fields.description: (Order date-time; Description cell blank in doc.)

response_fields.name: ordenttm
response_fields.description: (Order entry time; Description cell blank in doc.)

response_fields.name: extm
response_fields.description: (Exchange time; Description cell blank in doc.)

notes:
Returns a JSON ARRAY (not a single object) — one element per order-history report/event for the given norenordno. The sample shows the lifecycle most-recent-first: COMPLETE/Fill, OPEN/New, PENDING/PendingNew, PENDING/NewAck. Request jData fields (uid, norenordno) are both mandatory.

Doc inconsistency: in the response field tables, the trailing '*' required markers appear on prc, qty, trantype, dscqty, ret and amo — these markers are carried over verbatim from the order-placement request schema and are NOT meaningful for a read-only history response (every object returned simply contains whatever fields apply). They are recorded here as documented but should not be treated as 'required to send'.

Conditional response fields: bpprc only when product=B (Bracket); blprc and trailprc only when product=H and B (High Leverage and Bracket); amo only echoed when sent as 'Yes'.

Another doc inconsistency: the response-field TABLE lists orddttm / ordenttm / extm (Description cells blank), but the JSON SAMPLE instead carries norentm and exch_tm (and never shows orddttm/ordenttm/extm). The five fields uid, actid, orddttm, ordenttm, extm have blank Description cells in the source.

FAILURE response is a JSON object (not an array): stat='Not_Ok', request_time (Response received time), emsg (Error message). No literal failure JSON block is printed in the source; sample_failure_response is reconstructed from the failure field table (angle-bracket placeholders preserved). 'https://BaseURL/' in the curl is the doc's placeholder for the real base host https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI.
issues: The section's failure-response field table (stat/request_time/emsg) physically appears at the TOP of page 25, before the 'TRADE BOOK' title; it belongs to Single Order History (the 'in case of failure' lead-in is at the bottom of page 24). The field tables on the upper part of page 22 (amo, pp, ti, ls, tsym2/trantype2/qty2/prc2, tsym3/trantype3/qty3/prc3, fillshares2/3, avgprc2/3, plus a failure table) belong to the PRECEDING multi-leg/place-order section, NOT to Single Order History — only the 'SINGLE ORDER HISTORY' title sits at the very bottom of page 22, so the real section content begins on page 23. No literal failure JSON sample exists in the source; sample_failure_response is reconstructed from the field table. Verified against the page-23 and page-24 images: possible-value bindings prctyp=LMT, trantype=B/S, ret=DAY/EOS/IOC, amo=Yes, stat='Ok or Not_Ok' are correct; response-field count and ordering match the source (32 fields). The curl and the success-array JSON are verbatim from the cleaned page text (page-23/page-24), including the trailing space after "FZ00000", and the missing comma is not present (well-formed).
